etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "shake" + "-en" from Old English "sceacan" + "-en" (to move violently)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Move violently; experiencing disturbed unsettled and shaken disturbed supported by stability awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Move violently → disturbed unsettled → contemporary shaken disturbed and shaken unsettled that benefits from grounding growth
